---
title: "Module 05: Advanced Models"
---

## Learning objectives

- Name **random forests**, **gradient boosting** (e.g. XGBoost), and **neural networks** as the usual “strong tabular learners” after single trees.
- Explain the **capacity vs validation** tradeoff: more flexibility needs tighter resampling, more careful tuning, and clearer metrics.
- Describe how to **swap `mod_spec`** inside the same `tidymodels` workflow skeleton used on Tuesday.

## Key messages

- **Same recipe + resampling + metrics**, different **engine** — keeps student experiments comparable.
- **Never** skip the “what would break on small *n* penguins?” conversation when demoing huge models.

## Concept outline

### Random forests

- Bagged trees + feature subsampling; lower variance than one tree; often strong defaults.

### Boosting (XGBoost, etc.)

- Sequential error correction; strong performance with tuning burden; watch overfitting on small data.

### Neural networks (tabular)

- Flexible function class; needs careful scaling, architecture choices, and often more data than penguins provide — use as **API literacy**, not “win every Kaggle on 300 rows.”

### Practical teaching path

- Start from [Module 04](module-04-pipeline.qmd): replace `decision_tree` + `rpart` with `rand_forest` + `ranger` or `boost_tree` + `xgboost` on **`peng_cls`** or a train split; keep `tune_grid` discipline.

## Course links

- **Slides:** [Thursday — Advanced models and explainability](../slides/day-03-thursday.qmd)
- **Pipeline reference:** [Module 04](module-04-pipeline.qmd) (includes `_includes/tidymodels-pipeline.qmd`)
- **Notebooks:** any [penguin classification](../notebooks/penguins-classification.Rmd) notebook as the **prediction task** you reuse when swapping engines
- **Curriculum:** Thursday progression in [curriculum outline](../curriculum-outline.qmd)
- **Module library:** [Back to all modules](index.qmd)

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
## Draft / TODO

- Add **one** minimal `parsnip` spec table (engine, package, when to use) — static markdown table.
- Optional: link to official **tidymodels** tutorials for `xgboost` and `torch` when the course adds a lab machine with GPU.
:::
